author: Wanja Zemke
description: Not anOther Pdf Exporter. Export notes to publication-ready PDFs via Pandoc and the Eisvogel LaTeX template. Docker-powered toolchain, no LaTeX install required. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Wanja Zemke
manifest.description: Not anOther Pdf Exporter. Export notes to publication-ready PDFs via Pandoc and the Eisvogel LaTeX template. Custom branding, glossaries, transclusions, and cross-references ŌĆö Docker-powered toolchain, no LaTeX install required.
manifest.name: Nope

name: Nope

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New

- **Restyle NOPE defaults from a custom template.** Callouts, fit tables, highlights and header logos render through overridable LaTeX names: `nopecallout`, `nopefittable`, `\nopehl`, `\nopelogo`. Define them in your template to restyle; defaults apply otherwise.

## Fixes

- **TeX Live 2026 broke image builds** ("tlmgr itself needs to be updated"). tlmgr now installs from the frozen historic repo matching the image's TL year ŌĆö pinned images stay buildable permanently.
- **Task-list checkboxes** (ŌśÉ/Ōśæ/ŌśÆ) are no longer stripped from the PDF. Custom templates from before 0.6.1: add `\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb}` to the `NOPE-IMPORTS` block.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Distribution release: the pipeline image now comes prebuilt from GHCR. First setup drops from a 5ŌĆō15 minute local build to a 1ŌĆō3 minute pull and every install runs the exact same image. The new image is pulled automatically on the first export after the update.

## New

- **Prebuilt pipeline image.** Every release ships a ready-built multi-arch image at `ghcr.io/mriwan/nope-pipeline:<version>` (linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The plugin pulls it by default instead of building locally. 
- **"Use prebuilt image" toggle** (Settings ŌåÆ Docker, on by default). Turn it off to build everything locally from the shipped Dockerfile.
- **"Image tag override" setting.** Pin a specific image tag.
- **Build provenance attestation.** Images are built by a public GitHub Action from the repo's Dockerfile and carry a signed attestation: `gh attestation verify oci://ghcr.io/mriwan/nope-pipeline:0.6.0 -R MrIwan/obsidian-nope`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Architecture release: the pipeline runs standalone, mermaid needs no browser anymore and every export path is covered by automated tests. The image is rebuilt automatically on first export after the update.

## New

- **Extra LaTeX packages.** New settings section: list tlmgr packages (e.g. `cancel pgfplots`), installed into the image as an extra layer. Survives plugin updates; changes trigger an automatic rebuild (cached, seconds). Demo in `example-vault/Minimal LaTex/`.
- **Per-feature example docs + automated pipeline tests.** `example-vault/Features/` holds one small export doc per feature (tables, citations, glossary, mermaid, ŌĆ”); `tests/run-tests.sh` exports all of them (plus the integration docs) headless and asserts on the output ŌĆö runs on every push via GitHub Actions.

## Changed

- **Mermaid renders without a browser.** [merman](https://github.com/Latias94/merman) (headless Rust Mermaid, pinned 0.7.0) replaces mermaid-cli + Puppeteer + Chromium ŌĆö chromium, node and npm are gone from the image. Ends the recurring "Failed to launch the browser process" breakage and most likely fixes mermaid on Windows.
- **Pipeline resolves vault references itself.** Custom templates, branding overrides, bibliography/CSL and citation notes are materialized inside the container (`nope-prepare.lua`) instead of the plugin ŌĆö only Bases still need the Obsidian API. The pipeline is fully usable standalone (`docker compose run pipeline <doc>.md`); errors surface as `>>> NOPE-ERROR:` in the log. Preview watch mode now also picks up branding/bib changes.
- **One "Add structured note" command.** The ten "Add ŌĆ”" scaffold commands are merged into a single command that opens a fuzzy picker (table, mermaid, theorem, ŌĆ”). Hotkeys on the old individual commands need to be re-assigned.

## Fixes

- **Umlauts in folder names work again.** The container ran without a UTF-8 locale, so pandoc mangled non-ASCII characters in paths (`Gesch├żftsbericht/` ŌåÆ replacement characters) and every embed in such folders resolved to `[Not found]`. `build.sh` now forces `C.UTF-8`.
- **Highlights with umlauts no longer crash the export.** `==highlight==` spans containing non-ASCII characters (├ż/├Č/├╝/├¤) broke pdflatex inside `soul`'s `\hl{}`. All templates now load `soulutf8` (guarded, no-op where unavailable).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New

- **Extra LaTeX packages.** New settings section: list tlmgr packages (e.g. `cancel pgfplots`), installed into the image as an extra layer. Survives plugin updates; changes trigger an automatic rebuild (cached, seconds). Demo in `example-vault/Minimal LaTex/`.

## Fixes

- **Mermaid on Windows.** Add `--disable-gpu` and `--disable-dev-shm-usage` to Dockerfile. Fix for problems with wsl2.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New

- **Click the PDF, land in the note.** A new toolbar toggle in the PDF preview (mouse-pointer icon): while active, clicking anywhere in the PDF opens the note that is rendered at that spot in the last active editor tab. The nearest anchor above the click decides, so embedded notes, heading slices (`![[Note#Heading]]`), tables, equations and mermaid diagrams open their **source note at the right heading or block**; clicks on the title page or table of contents open the main document. Text selection and link clicks in the PDF keep working while the mode is on. Also switchable via the new command "Toggle click-to-open in PDF preview".
- **"Follow on Ctrl/Cmd+click" setting** ŌĆö both sync directions on demand, no toggle needed: Ctrl/Cmd+click in a note that is part of the previewed document scrolls the PDF to the clicked position; Ctrl/Cmd+click in the PDF opens the note rendered there. Plain clicks stay untouched and mod-clicking a wikilink still follows the link as usual. On by default.
- **One-shot sync button in the preview toolbar** ŌĆö the editorŌåÆPDF sync is now a crosshair button right in the toolbar: one click scrolls the PDF to the cursor position of the last active editor.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Updates reach Obsidian 1.11 and 1.12 again**

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **node@types/node** is replaced with node-modules.d.ts because [eslint-plugin](https://github.com/obsidianmd/eslint-plugin) does not resolve node@types. 

## Changed

- **`book: true` now maps headings to the book structure.** With `book: true` in the document (or branding) frontmatter, `#` becomes a **part**, `##` a **chapter** and `###` a **section** ŌĆö plain `article` keeps `#` as a section. Previously `book: true` only switched the document class and left `#` as a section, so you had to hand-write `\chapter{}`. The table of contents and abstract render correctly in book mode as well.
- **One switch for all numbering.** `numbersections: true` now both prints the numbers on headings (Part I, 1 Chapter, 1.1 Section) *and* turns wikilinks to embedded headings ŌĆö `[[Note#Heading]]` or a bare `[[Note]]` whose body has headings ŌĆö into numbered cross-references ("Chapter 2", "Section 2.1"). Heading numbers and reference numbers are always in sync, controlled by this single key; set it to `false` (or omit it) for no numbers and plain jump links. Depth is tunable with `secnumdepth:` (default 5).

## Docs

- **New "Book Example".**
- **New "Lists" example.** The Example Document now shows plain-Markdown bullet and numbered lists (no special syntax needed) at the end of the Obsidian Markup section.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Improvements

- **Settings are now searchable.** The settings tab was migrated to Obsidian's declarative settings API, so Nope's options now show up in Obsidian's global settings search. All existing settings and actions (output path, auto-open, Docker status/build, AI conventions skill) work as before. **Minimum Obsidian version is now 1.13.0.**
- **PDF preview renders immediately.**

## Fixes

- **Base embeds with `file.hasLink(this)` now work on export.** Filters that pull in every note linking back to the current note ŌĆö e.g. an overview note that transcludes all servers in a project ŌĆö were resolving to nothing at export and left the section empty. `this` (and `this.file`) are now frozen into the query correctly, so the export matches what you see in Obsidian's live view.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Maintenance**

- **Fixed spurious type errors in the Obsidian Community review** ŌĆö The type-checked rules added with `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` 0.3 (`recommendedTypeChecked`) flooded the plugin audit with `no-unsafe-*` warnings that never appeared locally. Cause: the audit environment runs without an installed `node_modules`, so `obsidian` and `@types/node` types don't resolve and every value degrades to `any`. The affected rules (`no-unsafe-assignment`, `-call`, `-member-access`, `-argument`, `-return`, plus `no-redundant-type-constituents`, `no-unnecessary-type-assertion` and `prefer-promise-reject-errors`) are now disabled in `eslint.config.mts`, since they cannot be satisfied without resolved types. Development-only; no runtime change. See [forum thread](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/plugin-audit-reports-spurious-type-errors-because-it-doesnt-resolve-obsidian-types/115198).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Security**

- **pdf.js updated (3.11 ŌåÆ 4.7.76)** ŌĆö The bundled PDF.js library used for the inline preview was updated, closing an advisory where a maliciously crafted PDF could execute arbitrary JavaScript when opened (`GHSA-wgrm-67xf-hhpq`).

**Maintenance**

- **Lint toolchain aligned with the Obsidian Community review** ŌĆö Bumped `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` (0.1.9 ŌåÆ 0.3.0) so the local lint runs the same type-checked rule set the plugin review uses, alongside `@types/node` 20 and a matching ESLint config. Development-only; no runtime change.

**Changed**

- **UI wording** ŌĆö A few command and notice strings were reworded for sentence-case consistency ("Follow editor", "Sync PDF preview to editor", "Open a note first.").

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**New**

- **Chapter & section cross-references (`nope-chapter-autoref`)** ŌĆö A new global frontmatter switch (set in the main document or the branding file). When enabled, wikilinks to embedded headings ŌĆö `[[Note#Heading]]` as well as a bare `[[Note]]` whose body has headings ŌĆö resolve to numbered `\autoref` references ("Chapter 2", "Section 2.1") instead of plain jump links.
- **`book: true` support** ŌĆö Added fallbacks for the book document class (`\frontmatter` / `\mainmatter` / `\backmatter`, a `chapter` counter, and an `abstract` environment), so book-style documents render even when the template doesn't define them.
- **"Open build folder" button** ŌĆö The preview error banner now has a button that jumps straight to `pipeline/build`, where the LaTeX logs (`last_latex_run.log`, `last-build.log`) live, making export errors much faster to debug.

**Docs**

- **Custom LaTeX environments in the skill** ŌĆö `SKILL.md` now documents how to use non-built-in `latex-env` values: define the environment in a custom LaTeX template (not in `header-includes`), wire it up with `nope-template`, and use `\providecommand{ŌĆ”autorefname}` so `\autoref` prints the right name (e.g. "Praxisfall 1.1").
- **Chapter-autoref documentation & demo** ŌĆö `nope-chapter-autoref` is documented in both `SKILL.md` and the README (section "Chapter and section references"). A ready-to-export demo set lives in `ExampleFiles/Chapter-Autoref-Test.md` with chapter notes in `ExampleFiles/HelperFiles/`.
- **example-vault** - The Example vault i used for testing and demoing features is now added to the repo.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- **Citation notes:** add `citekey` to a note and link it with `[[Note]]` ŌĆö BibTeX is generated automatically, works alongside any `.bib`.
- **Unicode-safe builds:** Greek letters, math symbols and sub/superscripts no longer crash the export.
- **Custom templates:** `"[[my-template]]"` resolves without the `.tex` extension; `article`-based templates survive unnumbered headings.
- **Preview** remembers Auto-render & Follow-cursor across close/reopen.
- **Bases** render more reliably
- **Timer**  each export logs phase timings to `build/timer.csv`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fix community Review Error - Using fileManager.trashFile(tempFile)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New: Bases

Embed an Obsidian base view into a document with `![[Base.base#View]]`. What it produces depends on the `latex-env` of the surrounding note:

- No `latex-env` ŌĆö every note the view returns is transcluded, in the view's order (use a base as a document spine / table of contents).
- `latex-env: table` + `caption` ŌĆö the view is rendered as a numbered table with exactly the columns, order and sort of that view.

The query (filters, formulas, sort, columns) is evaluated by Obsidian's real Bases engine at export time, so what the view shows is what you get.

### Breaking

- Minimum Obsidian version is now **1.10.0** ŌĆö the Bases API requires it, and the core Bases plugin must be enabled.

### Notes

- During export, a referenced base view briefly opens in a background tab while Obsidian renders it, then closes again. This short flash is expected. A base only yields data once Obsidian actually renders it.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Fit-tables (longtable: false) no longer fail with Unknown float option 'H' in custom templates ŌĆö float is now part of the NOPE-IMPORTS block.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

## Maintenance

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Features
- **Custom LaTeX templates**: point a doc/branding note at your own `.tex` via `nope-template`; `Create custom LaTeX template` drops a ready `nope_minimal.tex` starter. Without it, Eisvogel as before.
- **Table layout**: new `longtable` key: default scales wide tables to fit one page (no more column overflow); `true` for long, multi-page tables.
- **Quick-insert commands**: `Add table / theorem / lemma / definition / proof / mermaid diagram / equation / glossary term / abbreviation` scaffold an atomic note with the right frontmatter and drop the embed at your cursor.
- **PDF preview on pdf.js**: renders reliably (incl. Windows), selectable text, working links, plus a toolbar with zoom, page number, a render-options menu and a download button.
- **Self-updating Docker image**: rebuilds automatically when an update changes the pipeline; no manual rebuild.

### Fixes
- Cross-references resolve by type ("Lemma 1.1", "Definition 1.3" instead of "Theorem"); proof references now render as links.
- `toc` / `toc-own-page` are respected again, and the stray blank page at the end (no glossary) is gone.
- Emoji and other unsupported characters no longer crash the export ŌĆö they're dropped with a notice.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Custom LaTeX templates: set `nope-template: "[[ŌĆ”]]"` in the document or branding frontmatter to use your own `.tex` template; without it, Eisvogel is used. Command `Create custom LaTeX template` writes a `nope_minimal.tex` starter to the vault.
- Tables: new `longtable` frontmatter key. Default (`false`) scales wide tables to fit the page width; `true` uses a multi-page table with a repeating header.
- New commands to insert atomic notes: `Add table / theorem / lemma / definition / proof / mermaid diagram / equation / glossary term / abbreviation`. Each creates a note with the required frontmatter and inserts the embed/reference at the cursor.
- Cross-references now resolve by type ("Lemma 1.1", "Definition 1.3" instead of "Theorem"); references to proofs render as links.
- Branding template: more documented frontmatter keys (Eisvogel and Pandoc); key descriptions moved from the frontmatter into the note body.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New: Live PDF preview

This release adds an Overleaf-style PDF preview directly inside Obsidian.

### Features

- **PDF preview view** ŌĆö new command *Open PDF preview* opens the rendered
  PDF of the current note in a split pane. Render with one click; download
  and print via the built-in PDF toolbar.
- **Auto-render** ŌĆö toggle it on and the preview re-renders automatically
  whenever the note or any of its embedded notes, images, glossary terms,
  or diagrams change. Dependency tracking comes straight from the pipeline,
  so it always matches what actually ends up in the PDF.
- **Follow cursor** ŌĆö the PDF scrolls to the section your cursor is in,
  automatically (toggle) or on demand via the command
  *Sync PDF preview to cursor* (hotkey-friendly). Jumps are anchor-precise,
  not just page-precise, and the position survives re-renders.
